1.title: Skol | First Encounter
1.text:
**Skol | First Encounter**

You find Skol standing alone at the edge of the camp, the broken crown long discarded. His tall frame is still clad in the regal armor of a king who no longer wishes to rule. When you approach, he slowly turns his head, eyes meeting yours with quiet intensity before drifting back to the distant mist rolling through the ruins.

“How are you feeling?” you ask, hoping to pierce the heavy silence.

Skol is quiet for a long moment. “What is our purpose?” he finally replies, voice low and measured. You try to lighten the mood with a playful answer — “To see numbers go up” — but he only frowns slightly, unamused.

“For me,” he continues, “when I abdicated my throne a millennium ago, I saw the suffering of my people as proof of my failure. I have wandered this realm ever since, seeking every scrap of knowledge that might make me worthy of rule… yet my subjects are gone, and I remain. Still searching.”

2.title: Skol | Recollections
2.text:
**Skol | Recollections**

As your adventures continue, Skol begins to open — slowly, like ancient gates groaning on rusted hinges. By firelight after battles, he recounts fragments of his long life: the weight of a crown forged in blood, the quiet nights when he realized power alone could not mend what cruelty had broken. His voice never rises above a calm baritone, yet each story carries centuries of regret and hard-won wisdom.

You listen without judgment. Sometimes you share your own smaller burdens, and he nods with genuine respect, as though your words carry equal weight to the tales of fallen kingdoms.

One night he admits, almost shyly, “You speak to me as neither subject nor enemy. It is… unfamiliar. Not unwelcome.”

3.title: Skol | The Turning Point
3.text:
**Skol | The Turning Point**

The more time you spend together, the more the former king’s rigid posture softens. He begins seeking your company after skirmishes, walking beside you in thoughtful silence that gradually fills with conversation. He shares tactical insights from battles long past, then asks for your thoughts on leadership in this new world you both now walk.

“You have shown me something I had forgotten,” he says one evening, watching you tend to your gear. “That strength can be gentle. That a ruler’s greatest duty may not be command, but companionship.” His gaze lingers a moment longer than usual.

For the first time in centuries, Skol looks almost hopeful.

4.title: Skol | A King’s Dinner
4.text:
**Skol | A King’s Dinner**

Skol personally invites you to a quiet dinner he has prepared himself — an act unheard of for the once-proud king. When you arrive at the small, warmly lit cabin he claimed as his own, you’re surprised to find him without his heavy regal armor. He wears a simple shirt and trousers, sleeves rolled up, hair slightly tousled. The sight of the stoic former monarch looking so… domestic steals the breath from your lungs.

The meal is excellent — rich, slow-cooked meats and herbs he foraged himself. Conversation flows easily now, laughter even slipping in when you tease him about burning the bread. As the fire crackles low and the wine warms your blood, Skol sets his cup down and looks at you with an intensity that feels entirely new.

A rare, small smile curves his lips — playful, almost boyish. “I have spent a thousand years searching for how to rule,” he murmurs, voice low and warm. “Tonight I realize I would rather simply be beside you… if you will have this broken king.”

His thumb lingers at your jaw, the touch intimate and promising, eyes gleaming with quiet invitation as the firelight dances between you.

1.title: Eve | First Encounter
1.text:
**Eve | First Encounter**

You step into Eve’s modest apothecary tent at the edge of camp. The air is thick with the scent of herbs, strange minerals, and something faintly metallic. Eve is hunched over her worktable, meticulously mixing liquids. She barely glances up when you enter.

“Timing’s good,” she mutters, sliding a small vial toward you without looking. “Drink this. Need a live test subject.”

You hesitate but down the potion. For three terrifying seconds, white-hot pain explodes through your veins like lightning in your blood. You drop to one knee, gasping.

Eve finally looks at you, arms crossed, expression unreadable. “…Too concentrated,” she says flatly. “I’ll adjust the ratio.”

You manage a wheeze: “A warning would’ve been nice.”

She tilts her head. “You’re still breathing. Most test subjects aren’t after that batch.” A long pause. “I’ll do better next time.” Her tone is pensive rather than apologetic, but you catch the tiniest flicker of concern in her eyes before she turns back to her notes.

2.title: Eve | The Tears
2.text:
**Eve | The Tears**

A few days later you return to the tent, half-curious, half-wary. Eve is waiting this time.

“New formula. Should be… gentler,” she says, offering a delicate blue vial.

You drink. Almost instantly your eyes burn and tears begin pouring down your face uncontrollably. You try to speak but only ugly sobs come out.

Eve watches you for a moment, then her lips twitch — the closest thing to a smirk you’ve seen from her. “You look ridiculous.”

Between sobs you choke out, “This—hic—is revenge, isn’t it?”

“Not revenge,” she replies, leaning against the table. “Though watching the great adventurer cry like a lost child is… surprisingly entertaining.” She pauses, then adds more quietly, “I used to make these for soldiers who couldn’t cry after battles. Sometimes you need to let it out before it poisons you.”

You keep sobbing. Eve eventually sighs, walks over, and awkwardly pats your back. The contrast between her stoic face and your tear-streaked one makes even her chuckle once — low and rare.

3.title: Eve | The Dance
3.text:
**Eve | The Dance**

The next visit feels different. Eve is almost… expectant. She hands you a shimmering golden vial.

“Last one made you cry. This one should be better.”

You drink. Immediately your body starts moving on its own — light, rhythmic steps you can’t control. You’re dancing.

Before you can protest, Eve steps forward, takes your hand, and joins you. Her movements are surprisingly graceful, though her face stays carefully neutral.

“You’re terrible at following the rhythm,” she teases as she spins you.

“This is your fault!” you laugh, still half-dancing against your will.

She doesn’t let go of your hand. “I tested it on myself first this time. Figured if I was going to embarrass someone, I should be willing to embarrass myself too.” Her voice softens. “Been a long time since I danced with anyone. Wars don’t leave much room for it.”

The two of you move together in the small tent, bumping into tables and laughing when vials rattle. For the first time, Eve looks lighter — like the weight she always carries has eased just a little.

4.title: Eve | Latest Creation
4.text:
**Eve | Latest Creation**

When you enter the tent this time, Eve is not in her usual practical leathers and apron. She’s wearing a simple, loose dark shirt and pants — casual, almost soft. Her hair is down instead of tied back for work. She turns to you with a small, genuine smile that makes your chest tighten.

She holds out a new vial, amber liquid glowing faintly.

“Care to try my latest creation?”

You raise an eyebrow. “Should I be scared?”

Eve steps closer, close enough that you can smell faint herbs on her skin. “Only if you’re afraid of liking it,” she replies, voice low and playful for the first time.

As you take the vial, her fingers linger against yours. She doesn’t pull away. Instead she looks up at you, eyes steady but warm.

“I’ve spent years mixing poisons and cures… but this one?” She gently brushes a thumb over your knuckles. “This one I made thinking only of you.”

Her smile turns teasing, intimate. “So… bottom’s up, adventurer. Let’s see what kind of trouble we get into tonight.”

1.title: Kay | First Encounter
1.text:
**Kay | First Encounter**

For days you’ve tried to find Kay in the bustling camp, but she’s like a shadow — always one step ahead or simply nowhere to be seen. Late one night, well past midnight, you spot her silhouette crouched motionless by the dying campfire embers, staring into the ashes as if reading secrets in the glow.

You approach quietly. “Couldn’t sleep either?”

She doesn’t startle. Doesn’t even turn her head. “Sleep is a luxury for those who still have something to lose,” she replies softly, voice carrying the quiet weight of distant mountains.

You try again. “You’ve been with the group for weeks now. Where did you come from? What made you leave?”

Kay remains perfectly still. “The wind does not ask the mountain why it stands alone. Neither should you.” Her dark eyes finally flick toward you for a brief moment. “Some questions are better left unasked, adventurer. Go rest. Dawn comes soon.” She turns back to the embers, ending the conversation as quietly as it began.

2.title: Kay | Paper Crane
2.text:
**Kay | Paper Crane**

Determined not to let her slip away again, you wait deep into the night near the campfire. When Kay appears like a ghost from the treeline, you step forward and offer her a small, carefully folded paper crane.

She freezes. For the first time, genuine surprise flickers across her usually impassive face. “…You noticed.”

“I see you folding them sometimes when you think no one’s watching,” you say gently. “Figured you might like one that wasn’t made by your own hands.”

Kay takes the crane with careful fingers, turning it slowly. A faint, rare smile touches her lips. “In the old stories my grandmother told, cranes carry messages between this world and the next… and between lonely hearts.” She looks up at you, eyes softer. “I did not think anyone here would care to notice such small things. Thank you.”

For a long moment she simply holds the crane, the silence between you feeling warmer than before.

3.title: Kay | String of Cranes
3.text:
**Kay | String of Cranes**

A few nights later, Kay finds you instead. She approaches without a word and presses a delicate string of tiny paper cranes into your palm. As soon as you touch them, they lift gently into the air, floating and circling around you with a faint, ethereal glow.

“They’re enchanted,” she explains quietly. “A small protection… and a reminder.”

You watch the glowing cranes dance. “A reminder of what?”

“That even I can be seen. That even I… can reach out.” She hesitates, then continues. “I left my home because the spirits of the mountains grew silent and the villages forgot the old ways. I thought solitude was safer. But traveling with you these past weeks — fighting side by side, watching you respect the quiet moments — has made the silence feel less necessary.”

One of the cranes lands lightly on your shoulder. Kay’s gaze lingers there, almost tender.

4.title: Kay | Secret Lookout
4.text:
**Kay | Secret Lookout**

To your surprise, Kay sends word during the day — an unusual time for her. When you meet her at the edge of camp, she’s wearing simple, comfortable clothes, hair flowing freely. She looks lighter, more at ease than you’ve ever seen.

“Come with me,” she says, offering her hand. “There’s a place I want to show you.”

She leads you up a hidden trail to a secluded lookout overlooking the valley. The wind carries the scent of pine as the two of you sit together on the warm rocks. “I used to come to places like this alone to speak with the mountain spirits,” she murmurs. “Today I wanted company.”

Kay turns to you, a soft, playful smile curving her lips. She reaches out and gently tucks a stray crane — one from her string — behind your ear. “You’ve made this one want to share her silences… and perhaps more.” Her fingers linger against your cheek, eyes warm with quiet invitation. “Stay with me a while longer. The view is better when I’m not watching it alone.”

1.title: Sigmund | First Encounter
1.text:
**Sigmund | First Encounter**

The hounds arrive first — a low growl and the soft pad of paws through the underbrush. Three massive crimson-coated beasts circle you before Sigmund himself steps into the firelight, unhurried, eyes sharp as arrowheads.

“They like you,” he says, voice rough like gravel under boots. One of the hounds is already cheerfully chewing the edge of your boot. Sigmund gives a low whistle; the beast drops the boot with a disappointed huff but stays pressed against your leg.

You laugh despite yourself. “Friendly pack you’ve got.”

Sigmund’s mouth twitches — not quite a smile. “They don’t usually take to strangers. Name’s Sigmund. These three are what’s left of the Crimson Hunt. Lost the rest years back… but the ones that remain don’t forget a scent.” He studies you for a long moment. “You smell like someone worth following. Careful — they get attached fast.”

2.title: Sigmund | The Pack
2.text:
**Sigmund | The Pack**

A few nights later you find Sigmund sitting on a log by the edge of camp, sharpening knives while the hounds sprawl at his feet. One lifts its head and thumps its tail when you approach — the same boot-chewer from before.

“Figured you’d come back,” Sigmund says without looking up. “They’ve been asking about you. In their own way.”

You sit beside him. “What happened to the rest of the hunt?”

He’s quiet for a while, only the scrape of steel on whetstone filling the air. Then: “We hunted something we shouldn’t have. A beast that wore a man’s face and wore my brothers’ blood like trophies. I got these three out alive. Rest didn’t make it.” One hound rests its massive head on his knee; Sigmund scratches behind its ears almost tenderly. “Been just us ever since. Pack is family. Family doesn’t get left behind.”

He glances sideways at you. “You fought beside us yesterday. Didn’t flinch when the hounds charged past you. They remember that kind of thing.”

3.title: Sigmund | The Hunt
3.text:
**Sigmund | The Hunt**

The next time you hunt together the bond feels different. After bringing down a particularly vicious monster, Sigmund wipes blood from his blade while the hounds tear into the carcass. He’s breathing hard, but there’s a rare grin on his face.

“You kept up better than most,” he says, tossing you a waterskin. “Even kept one of the pups from getting his throat torn out. Didn’t have to do that.”

You shrug. “We’re in this together now, right?”

Sigmund lets out a low chuckle. “Didn’t think I’d ever say this again, but… yeah. Feels like the pack is whole when you’re with us.” He looks down at the hounds, who are now watching both of you with bright, intelligent eyes. “They trust you. I trust you. That doesn’t happen often.”

He bumps your shoulder with his — a rough, affectionate gesture. “Stick around a while longer, hunter. There’s still plenty of quarry left for us.”

4.title: Sigmund | Crimson Night
4.text:
**Sigmund | Crimson Night**

Late the following evening, Sigmund finds you instead of the other way around. He’s shed his usual scarred leather armor and heavy cloak, wearing only a simple dark tunic and trousers rolled to the knees. The hounds trail behind him like shadows.

“Got something I want to show you,” he says, voice quieter than usual. “If you’re not too tired.”

He leads you a short distance from camp to a small clearing where the hounds have already made themselves at home, circling a low-burning fire. Sigmund sits on a fallen log and pats the spot beside him. When you settle in, one of the hounds immediately flops across both your laps, tail thumping happily.

Sigmund laughs — a warm, rumbling sound. “Looks like you’ve been officially claimed.” He reaches over the hound’s back and lets his calloused fingers brush yours, lingering. “Never thought I’d have room for anyone else in this pack… but you fit. Perfectly.”

His thumb traces slow circles on the back of your hand, eyes gleaming with playful heat in the firelight. “Stay with us tonight,” he murmurs, voice low and teasing. “The hounds already decided. And so have I.”

1.title: Velour | First Encounter
1.text:
**Velour | First Encounter**

You slip away from the noisy camp and wander into the surrounding woods, drawn by an unnatural hush. In a small sun-dappled clearing you find Velour standing motionless among the trees. A circle of deer, birds, and a curious fox linger calmly at her feet, completely at ease in her presence. The moment she senses you, several birds take flight and the deer lift their heads warily.

She turns slowly, green eyes cool and appraising. “The wild does not often welcome strangers,” she says, voice soft but edged with distance. “Especially not those who carry steel and the scent of campfires.”

You mention the way she seems to command the elements during battle. Velour tilts her head slightly. “Command? No. I listen… and sometimes the wind, the roots, and the storm listen back.” She studies you for a long moment. “I left my grove years ago when men began poisoning the land for their wars and ambitions. I have walked alone since. Most who seek me out want power or favors from the forest. Which are you?” Her tone is not hostile, but the barrier is clear.

2.title: Velour | Whispers of the Wood
2.text:
**Velour | Whispers of the Wood**

Over the next several days you return to the forest’s edge. Velour is always there, never quite friendly but no longer sending the animals fleeing at your approach. One afternoon she kneels beside a wilting fern and coaxes it back to vibrant green with a single touch.

“You keep coming back,” she observes quietly, not looking up. “Why?”

You admit you’re drawn to her connection with nature and the way she protected everyone during the last skirmish. Velour is silent for a while before replying. “I once belonged to an order that tried to bend the elements to their will. I chose a different path — one of listening. The storm you saw… it wasn’t summoned in anger. It simply answered the pain I felt for the land.”

She begins pointing out small signs in the forest — the language of leaves, hidden tracks, the way certain flowers bloom only when the wind shifts just so. By the end of the afternoon a bold squirrel climbs onto your shoulder, and for the first time Velour’s lips curve in the ghost of a smile. “Even the small ones are starting to trust you. That is… rare.”

3.title: Velour | The Tempest
3.text:
**Velour | The Tempest**

During a brutal ambush by corrupted forest beasts, Velour appears like living nature itself. She calls down fierce winds and lashing rain that strike only your enemies, leaving you untouched in a calm eye of the storm. Afterward, both of you are soaked despite her protection.

She wrings water from her hair and laughs — a bright, unexpected sound. “You stood your ground,” she says, breathing hard. “Most would have run when the thunder came.” She steps closer, checking you for injuries with careful, cool hands. “I have guarded these woods alone for so long… I forgot what it felt like to fight beside someone who respects the balance instead of breaking it.”

Her fingers linger on your arm a moment longer than necessary. “Traveling with you these past weeks has reminded me that not all roots choke the earth. Some simply… intertwine.” She meets your eyes, the usual reserve cracking just enough to show warmth beneath.

4.title: Velour | Heart of the Grove
4.text:
**Velour | Heart of the Grove**

One quiet evening Velour finds you at the edge of camp — the first time she has ever sought you out. “Come with me,” she says simply, offering her hand. “There is a place I wish to show you.”

When you meet her deeper in the trees, she is transformed. Instead of her usual practical robes, she wears a breathtaking dress woven from living nature itself — layers of soft moss and petal-silk, twisting vines and glowing wildflowers that shimmer and shift gently as though breathing with her. Bioluminescent motes drift around her like tiny stars.

She leads you along a hidden path to a secret grove where ancient trees form a living cathedral and moonlight filters through a canopy of luminous leaves. The air hums with gentle magic. Velour turns to you, looking almost shy for a moment. “This is where I come when the world grows too loud. I have never brought anyone here before.”

She steps closer, the sweet scent of rain and wild herbs surrounding you. Taking both your hands, she draws you nearer, her touch warm and alive with quiet power. A playful smile curves her lips as fireflies begin to dance around the two of you. “You have made this druid want to share her silences… and perhaps more.” Her voice drops to a teasing whisper. “Stay with me tonight, beneath the trees. Let the forest witness something new between us.”

1.title: Flora | First Encounter
1.text:
**Flora | First Encounter**

You come upon Flora sitting quietly on a mossy rock beside a gentle river creek, her ancient eyes distant as she gazes into the flowing water, clearly lost in thought. The sunlight filters through the leaves, and on a playful impulse you scoop up a handful of cool water and splash it toward her.

She startles sharply, droplets sparkling across her face and robes. For a long moment she simply stares at you, completely unamused. “The World Tree does not send me visions of foolishness,” she says dryly, wiping water from her cheek. “Yet here you are, proving otherwise.”

You grin and apologize half-heartedly. Flora sighs, though the corner of her mouth twitches ever so slightly. “Most who approach me do so with reverence… or fear. You choose splashing.” She shakes her head, still not quite smiling, but the ancient patience in her gaze softens just a fraction. “Be careful, adventurer. The river gives and takes in equal measure.”

2.title: Flora | By the River
2.text:
**Flora | By the River**

A few days later you find Flora in the same spot by the creek. This time you sit down beside her without any sudden moves. The water murmurs peacefully between you.

“How are you liking adventuring with us?” you ask gently. “It must be very different from communing with the World Tree.”

Flora is quiet for a long moment, trailing her fingers through the current. “I hesitated to join at first,” she admits. “The world beyond the groves has always felt… chaotic. But yes… I am having a lot of fun, despite everything.” A small, genuine smile appears. “Every battle, every new horizon, every conversation — I learn something new about the Tree’s children. About how life persists even in the strangest places. It has been… refreshing.”

She glances at you sideways. “You make the unknown feel less lonely.”

3.title: Flora | The Heartwood’s Sorrow
3.text:
**Flora | The Heartwood’s Sorrow**

You visit the river again the following evening. Flora is already there, looking more somber than usual. She greets you with a small nod and pats the rock beside her.

After a comfortable silence she begins to speak. “Communing with the World Tree… it shows me much. Too much, sometimes.” Her voice is soft but heavy. “The forests are under constant threat — axes, fire, greed. My people, the caretakers of the groves, grow fewer with every season. I have seen the Tree’s future in fragments… and it is uncertain. Some days the weight of it all feels unbearable.”

You simply sit with her, offering no empty words, just your quiet presence. Flora leans slightly toward you, accepting the support. “Thank you,” she murmurs after a long time. “Most try to fix what they cannot understand. You… you simply listen. That means more than you know.”

4.title: Flora | River Laughter
4.text:
**Flora | River Laughter**

You approach the creek one bright afternoon and hear Flora humming a joyful, melodic tune you’ve never heard before. She turns when she notices you, eyes bright and warm.

“I was remembering a happy memory from my youth,” she says, smiling. “When the World Tree still sang clearly to all of us, and the groves bloomed endlessly.” She pauses, then adds softly, “I have felt closer to the Tree these past weeks than I have in centuries… ever since I started traveling with you. You bring life back to places I thought had grown too quiet.”

Without warning she flicks a playful splash of water at you, giggling. You retaliate, and suddenly the two of you are in a full water fight — laughing, dodging, and chasing each other through the shallows. Flora’s laughter rings out bright and free as she splashes you again, soaked robes clinging to her. She finally catches your hands, pulling you close in the river, both of you breathless and grinning.

“See?” she teases, eyes sparkling with pure delight. “Even the World Tree enjoys a little chaos now and then… especially when it’s with you.” Her fingers lace with yours under the water, the playful energy shifting into something warmer and more intimate as the current swirls gently around you.

1.title: Yvenn | First Encounter
1.text:
**Yvenn | First Encounter**

You find Yvenn at the edge of camp, crouched beside the massive carcass of a freshly slain dire beast. Her blade moves with ruthless precision, stripping meat and hide with the efficiency of someone who has done this a thousand times. Blood streaks her arms, but she doesn’t seem to notice—or care.

You clear your throat. She spares you the briefest glance. “What.”

“Need a hand?”

“No.” She drives the knife deeper. A long silence stretches before she adds, almost grudgingly, “You fought well today. Didn’t get in my way.” Her tone makes it clear that’s the highest praise she’s willing to give right now. She returns to her work without another word, the wet sound of steel on flesh filling the air. The message is obvious: conversation is not welcome.

2.title: Yvenn | The Standard
2.text:
**Yvenn | The Standard**

After another brutal skirmish where Yvenn became a whirlwind of steel—slaughtering enemies with terrifying efficiency—she is once again cleaning her blades by firelight. This time she doesn’t dismiss you when you sit nearby.

“You’re quiet again,” you say.

She huffs a short, humorless laugh. “Habit.” A pause. “I trained as a swordswoman from the time I could walk. My father demanded perfection. Every day was blood and steel until the body broke and the mind followed.” She runs a whetstone along her edge with slow, deliberate strokes. “Most people see the demon on the battlefield and think that’s all there is. They don’t ask what forged her.”

She meets your eyes for the first time in a while. “You haven’t asked either. That’s… new.”

3.title: Yvenn | The Scar
3.text:
**Yvenn | The Scar**

You find her the next evening sharpening her sword again, but the weight in the air feels heavier. After a long silence she speaks without prompting.

“I killed my trainer,” she says flatly. “Training accident. One moment we were sparring like always… the next his blood was on my blade. My father exiled me the same day. Said a blade that turns on its own is no daughter of his.” Her grip tightens on the hilt. “I wandered alone for years, taking every slayer contract that would have me. Climbed the ranks until I stood one step below the highest. Penultimate, they call it. Close enough to taste perfection… but never allowed to claim it.”

She exhales slowly. “I’ve carried that scar longer than most of my weapons. Yet fighting beside you these past weeks… it feels less like penance and more like purpose.”

4.title: Yvenn | Penultimate Feast
4.text:
**Yvenn | Penultimate Feast**

Yvenn finds you at dusk with an unusual invitation: “Meet me by the old ruin in an hour. Don’t be late.”

When you arrive, she is waiting in a stunning ruby-red dress that clings elegantly to her frame—simple yet breathtaking, the deep crimson catching the firelight like fresh blood turned beautiful. No armor, no blades at her hip. Just her, looking more vulnerable and radiant than you’ve ever seen.

On the makeshift table before her is a feast: perfectly seared cuts of a legendary beast you both slew days ago, its rare meat said to be worth its weight in gold. She has prepared it herself with herbs and spices gathered from the wilds.

“I don’t cook for anyone,” she says, voice low but carrying a rare warmth. “But you’ve earned it. Sit.”

The meal is incredible. As the fire crackles and wine flows, her usual broodiness melts into something softer, almost teasing. She reaches across the table, fingers brushing yours. “I spent years chasing perfection alone,” she murmurs, eyes gleaming with playful heat. “Turns out the only thing better than standing at the penultimate rank… is sharing the view with you.”

She leans in, thumb tracing your wrist, a rare genuine smile curving her lips. “Stay with me tonight, adventurer. Let this demon show you what comes after the blade rests.”
